title: Immediate, Not Gradual
orientation: An Abolitionists Rising pillar page arguing that abortion must be immediately abolished as the murder it is, not regulated incrementally, because incrementalism codifies iniquity, submits to courts rather than to God, and (per Elizabeth Heyrick) lulls the conscience of the church into tolerating the very horror it claims to oppose.
audiences: pastor weighing whether to support immediate abolition bills
newcomer asking what makes abolitionists different from pro-lifers
skeptic who thinks 'something is better than nothing'

questions.question: What is the deepest difference between abolitionists and pro-lifers, according to AR?
questions.audience: newcomer asking what makes abolitionists different from pro-lifers
questions.answer: It is not primarily a strategic disagreement but a question of ultimate authority: abolitionists submit to God, while the pro-life movement effectively submitted to the Supreme Court for fifty years and continues to submit to political and cultural pressure rather than to Christ.
questions.quote: Beneath the differences in legislative strategies pursued by abolitionists and pro-lifers is a difference in our ultimate allegiances. Abolitionists submit to God as our Ultimate authority while pro-lifers submitted to the Supreme Court as their Ultimate authority for 50 years.

questions.question: How does the article characterize the pro-life response to courts that legalize child sacrifice?
questions.answer: It calls the pro-life response submission rather than defiance, citing leaders like Joe Pojman who said opposing SCOTUS would be like defying gravity, and Scott Klusendorf who treated Roe and Casey as settled dictates from on high.
questions.quote: This submission is unconditional, as explained by Joe Pojman of Texas Alliance for Life who told the Austin Chronicle in April of 2019 that he opposed abolishing abortion in Texas because “We could no sooner defy SCOTUS than we could defy the force of gravity.”

questions.question: Beyond Scripture, what legal authority do abolitionists invoke against Roe and the courts?
questions.answer: The Constitution itself: equal protection bars federal, state, and subordinate governments from permitting the murder of any class of human beings, making Roe sedition against the Constitution and making nullification by states the only legally appropriate response.
questions.quote: The federal government, the states, and all other subdivisions thereof are Constitutionally barred from providing unequal protection of the law. They cannot allow the murder of certain classes of human beings meaning Roe was sedition against the Constitution.

questions.question: Why does AR call incremental pro-life bills 'iniquitous decrees'?
questions.audience: pastor weighing whether to support immediate abolition bills
questions.answer: Because a bill banning abortion at, for example, 20 weeks explicitly writes into law the legality of murdering younger preborn children, which Isaiah 10 condemns; iniquity is iniquity regardless of how it compares to a worse status quo.
questions.quote: A law which bans abortion at 20 weeks may sound good at first, but such a bill explicitly codifies into the law the acceptability of murdering babies younger than 20 weeks. There can be no serious debate - this is an iniquitous decree.

questions.question: What is wrong with arguing 'this incremental bill is less iniquitous than the status quo, so support it'?
questions.audience: skeptic who thinks 'something is better than nothing'
questions.answer: The article identifies that argument as moral relativism: God's word contains no exception permitting Christians to write iniquity into law on the basis that it is comparatively less iniquitous than something else.
questions.quote: But there are no exceptions in God's word which allow us to write iniquity into law so long as it's less iniquitous than something else. Iniquity is iniquity, and there's no question that incremental bills are iniquitous. To support iniquity because of its relative degree of iniquity compared to something else is moral relativism, an un-Godly ethical system to put it lightly.

questions.question: Can a person consistently embrace both immediatism and incrementalism?
questions.answer: No. The article insists the two are diametrically opposed and that incrementalism fatally undermines any attempt at immediate abolition, citing Elizabeth Heyrick on the slavery analogue.
questions.quote: Some pro-lifers claim to support both regulation and abolition, but immediatism and incrementalism cannot be embraced simultaneously. They are diametrically opposed to one another, with incrementalism fatally undermining attempts at immediate abolition.

questions.question: How does Elizabeth Heyrick describe the strategic effect of gradualism on the conscience of reformers?
questions.audience: newcomer asking what makes abolitionists different from pro-lifers
questions.answer: She argues gradualism breeds gradual indifference: the slaveholder knew that as long as abolitionists could be persuaded to demand gradual rather than immediate emancipation, public outrage would dissipate and the system would be safe.
questions.quote: The slave holder knew very well that his prey would be secure, so long as the abolitionists could be cajoled into a demand for gradual instead of immediate abolition. He knew very well, that the contemplation of a gradual emancipation, would beget a gradual indifference to emancipation itself.

questions.question: What does Heyrick name as the spiritual source behind the gradualist strategy?
questions.audience: pastor weighing whether to support immediate abolition bills
questions.answer: Satan himself: she calls gradual abolition 'the very masterpiece of satanic policy,' a transformation of the prince of slaveholders into an angel of light who deceives even the wise and good with humane-sounding arguments for delay.
questions.quote: But this GRADUAL ABOLITION, has been the grand marplot of human virtue and happiness; the very masterpiece of satanic policy. By converting the cry for immediate, into gradual emancipation, the prince of slave holders, ‘transformed himself, with astonishing dexterity, into an angel of light,’ and thereby ‘deceived the very elect.’

questions.question: How does AR map Heyrick's diagnosis onto the modern pro-life movement?
questions.audience: newcomer asking what makes abolitionists different from pro-lifers
questions.answer: Millions of Christians have been cajoled into ending abortion gradually, which has produced gradual indifference; apart from a few flashes of outrage, the church is largely silent, though that is beginning to change.
questions.quote: It’s hard to read Heyrick’s work without seeing a depiction of the pro-life movement. Millions of Christians have been cajoled into gradually ending abortion which causes a gradual indifference to ending abortion itself.

questions.question: What does the article claim would actually end abortion?
questions.audience: pastor weighing whether to support immediate abolition bills
questions.answer: A faithful, uncompromising delineation of abortion's horrors by the hundreds of millions of Christians in the country; settling for ultrasound laws and waiting periods while calling small percentage reductions a win guarantees abortion will never end.
questions.quote: A faithful, uncompromising delineation of the horrors of abortion by the hundreds of millions of Christians in this country would bring abortion to an end. But if those millions of Christians settle for Right to Life, and Right to Life-allied centrist Republicans passing ultrasound laws and waiting periods which reduce abortion by one or two or five percent and call that winning, there will never be an end to abortion

questions.question: How does the article summarize the contrast between abolitionist and pro-life demands?
questions.answer: Abolitionists demand abolition because abortion is murder; pro-life leaders demand incremental regulation, conceded Roe's legitimacy, and treat abortion as a form of health care to be limited rather than as murder to be outlawed.
questions.quote: Abolitionists demand abortion’s abolition. Pro-life leaders demand abortion’s incremental regulation. Pro-lifers conceded Roe’s legitimacy. Abolitionists called Roe what it is: illegitimate, wicked, and was never binding on the states. Pro-lifers regulate abortion as if it were health care. Abolitionists demand abortion be immediately abolished because it’s murder.

questions.question: Whose arguments against incrementalism does the article point readers to for further study?
questions.audience: skeptic who thinks 'something is better than nothing'
questions.answer: T. Russell Hunter at the 2021 'Pro-Life is Not Enough Conference,' where he also addresses 'Smashmouth Incrementalism,' a term coined by Moscow, ID pastor Doug Wilson.
questions.quote: Check out T. Russell Hunter taking on the Incrementalist approach at the "Pro-Life is Not Enough Conference" in 2021. He also takes on "Smashmouth Incrementalism", which is a term coined by Moscow, ID pastor Doug Wilson.

related.title: No Exceptions
related.reason: Companion AR pillar page arguing the same anti-compromise logic applied to so-called exceptions in abolition bills.

related.title: Biblical, Not Secular
related.reason: Companion AR pillar page grounding the immediatist position in Scripture rather than pragmatic or secular reasoning, directly extending this article's appeal to God as Ultimate authority.

related.title: Abolitionist, Not Pro-Life
related.reason: Companion AR pillar page that develops the abolitionist-vs-pro-life distinction this article opens with.

related.title: Immediate, Not Gradual Abolition of Abortion
related.reason: Long-form Free The States essay by James Silberman building the same immediatist case and quoting Elizabeth Heyrick at length.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Provides the concrete enumerated case against incremental pro-life legislation that this pillar page summarizes.

related.title: King Josiah's Smashmouth Abolitionism
related.reason: Directly engages the 'Smashmouth Incrementalism' framing of Doug Wilson that this article names at the end.

related.title: Four Problems With David Barton's Incrementalism Apologia
related.reason: Rebuts a prominent historical-Christian defense of incrementalism, complementing this page's Heyrick-rooted historical argument.
